title: Lankesteriana inversa
intro: Lankesteriana inversa, also known as the 'Peruvian Ginger', is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Peruvian Andes, characterized by its unique and attractive foliage. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Lankesteriana inversa include gingerols, shogaols, and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments used to treat skin conditions and wounds.

It belongs to the genus Lankesteriana, which is a small group of orchids. Lankesteriana inversa is an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants such as trees for support.
